notes: Visible glitches in 4k, no problem in 1920x1080, runs well and fast. Save/load works. However just tried to continue level2 ("the journey so far - death sought crowfather...") and it crashes after selecting the save, either before or after the main menu. title: Darksiders II Deathinitive Edition

notes: This is an interesting one. To talk about challenge, here we go: Game runs with Vsync on and shadows and antialiasing off: but poorly at that. Between 26 and 60 fps. I average 35fps. To avoid the crashing everyone is affected by I have increased my open file limits but the strangest workaround by far is this next one. I start a new game from the main menu and as soon as the game lets me, pause and enter options. Then I go back to the main menu and load the save holding my progress. That is the only consistent way I know to mitigate the crashing. Start a dummy new game, pause and enter game options, then load actual savegame.
title: Darksiders II Deathinitive Edition

notes: Game works well most of the time, but framerate is joke (I got 40 most of the time, it's not that noticeable tho). DX11 mode doesn't work, so no Vulkan for us, I suppose. To prevent game constantly crashes you need to disable esync, but even then game like to crash a lot, so don't even try to alt-tab -- it will result in crash. To prevent graphical issues you need to disable anti-aliasing. I'm also playing in GL mode, to enable it you need to write in launch options  %command% -gl.

title: Darksiders II Deathinitive Edition

notes:
I think the problem is when the game loads or saves and NOT in proton config.
You have to create two savings, one will be a "fictitious save" and one will be a "good save".

So, step by step:
1) In set launch option copy this: PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 %command%
2) Start the game and wait to be in the Main Screen;
3) Load the "fictitious save" and without doing anything exit it returning to the main screen of the game;
4) Load your "good save" which is the one you'll really play with.

Remember to do this * every time * you want to start and play Darksiders II: Deathinitive Edition.
title: Darksiders II Deathinitive Edition

notes: When I just started the game it ran flawlessly: I could load a save game and play for about 15 minutes. No further configuration, most settings set to maximum (no ambient occlusion). I remembered from previous tests that loading save games was an issue back then and so I tried to exit the game and reload it. That did not work at all. I have since been trying to get back into the game but sadly it crashes most of the time when I try to load a saved game. I also tried to start a new game, but that also crashed shortly after playing the first two intro sequences (which I skipped)

title: Darksiders II Deathinitive Edition

notes:
Sometimes crashes on loading a save (during recap) and when pressing Continue. As previous reports suggest, opening the options can help with this, but is by no means guaranteed to work.

The game has crashed seemingly at random during gameplay with little to no warning, and there appears to be no particular pattern to this.
Although the game runs perfectly at 60FPS in many areas, there are some places where the framerate can dramatically drop and be wildly inconsistent to the point that it's just not feasibly to play.
This has been observed at "The Maw" (framerate could fling between 15-60FPS) and "The Tree Of Life" (hitting lows of around 10FPS) of the places I tested. However, other locations (notably "Shadow Gorge" and "The Spine") run perfectly without incident despite being rather large and detailed regions.

Disabling ESync gave performance that was too poor for normal play, and no graphics options (inclusive of VSync) seemed not to improve results any (changing resolution was not tested).

The startup video would also sometime be randomly garbled, but this never impacted gameplay, which has been visually perfect.
title: Darksiders II Deathinitive Edition

notes: Works fine out of the box on Proton. I installed D9VK and it worked great too and a much better frame rate than with WineD3D, even when testing it with the new ACO compiler.
title: Darksiders II Deathinitive Edition

notes: Generally playable, though highly unstable with a tendency to crash when loading a game. Opting into the beta greatly improves stability, but introduces very distracting flickering in the sky. Controller rumble does not seem to work.
title: Darksiders II Deathinitive Edition

notes: Seems to work fine with the latest proton update. No chrash after the game has loaded and started, no lag and it run smooth, I only tested it for some minutes. I did nothing just plug and play
title: Darksiders II Deathinitive Edition

notes: Opting into the beta version fixes issues with the game crashing while loading a save. Strobe light sun can be fixed by using Proton 4.11-1 and enabling D9VK ("PROTON_USE_D9VK=1 %command%" in launch options). Everything seems fully functional after these fixes except for controller rumble.
title: Darksiders II Deathinitive Edition

notes: Used to be unplayable on older Proton versions (crashes and stuttering). No problems on Proton 4.11-2 and 9VK enabled ("PROTON_USE_D9VK=1 %command%" in launch options).
title: Darksiders II Deathinitive Edition

notes: Did a full play through. Performance was okay, most of the time unstable frame rate above 40 and occasional stutters. Had to enable D9VK and set shadows and AA to medium. Used the stable branch, as the beta one has performance problems. When encountering issues switching to beta and switching back to stable is possible.
title: Darksiders II Deathinitive Edition
